NACA 4424 is real airfoil: 4% camber, 40% camber location, 24% thickness. Commonly used for blade root due to thickness
NACA 4412 is real airfoil: 4% camber, 40% camber location, 12% thickness. Commonly used for blade tip
Re=1M is standard for wind turbine airfoil analysis. QBlade documentation confirms support for this value
QBlade is real open-source wind turbine simulation software (APL license). Environment documentation confirms v0.96.2 64-bit CE

privileged_info_summary: The task requires reconstructing a wind turbine blade design called 'Legacy-V1' in QBlade simulation software using two real NACA airfoils (NACA 4424 for root, NACA 4412 for tip). The blade specifications consist of 5 stations spanning 40m with specific chord and twist distributions. QBlade v0.96.2 is confirmed as real, open-source software available on Linux. NACA airfoils are real, standard wind turbine profile designations. Reynolds number 1,000,000 is a standard value for wind turbine aerodynamic analysis. The specific 'Legacy-V1' blade geometry values (chord/twist at each station) appear to be synthetic test data without matching any publicly documented wind turbine, though the values are physically realistic for a small horizontal-axis wind turbine blade. Manufacturing tolerances (±0.05m chord, ±0.15° twist) are plausible but could not be independently verified against IEC standards.
